features.description: Create the Xcode project, app entry point, empty NavigationSplitView skeleton, Info.plist, bundle identifier nl.rb2.kite, min system version macOS 15, sandbox disabled in entitlements, Sign to Run Locally identity, SwiftLint + SwiftFormat config, Swift Testing target.
expectedBehavior: Kite.xcodeproj opens in Xcode 16 without warnings.
Debug build produces Kite.app that launches to an empty three-pane NavigationSplitView.
swift test (empty suite) exits 0.
swiftformat --lint and swiftlint --strict exit 0.
Info.plist contains required keys (bundle id, version, category, min system version, high-res capable).
verificationSteps: xcodebuild -scheme Kite -configuration Debug build
plutil -p Kite.app/Contents/Info.plist and confirm required keys
Launch the app and observe empty split view renders

features.description: Implement Git.run(args:cwd:) async throws -> GitResult and Git.stream(args:cwd:) -> AsyncThrowingStream<GitEvent, Error> with cancellation support. Add GitError, ErrorClassifier mapping known stderr patterns, and environment hardening (GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT=0, GIT_OPTIONAL_LOCKS=0, LC_ALL=C). Verify git availability at startup.
expectedBehavior: Git.run executes /usr/bin/git with given args and cwd, returns stdout/stderr/exit.
Git.stream yields GitEvent cases (stdoutLine, stderrLine, progress, completed) as they arrive.
Task cancellation terminates the child process via Process.terminate().
ErrorClassifier maps `fatal: Authentication failed`, `non-fast-forward`, `no upstream`, `Your local changes`, etc. to typed GitError cases.
Missing or outdated git produces a clear startup error.
verificationSteps: swift test --filter GitRunTests
swift test --filter ErrorClassifierTests
Manual: run Git.run(["--version"]) from a unit test and confirm exit 0

features.description: Implement porcelain parsers: branch-list parser (null-delimited format fields), log parser (topo-ordered, null-delimited fields, parent arrays), status parser (--porcelain=v2 --branch -z), for-each-ref parser, unified-diff parser, progress parser (splits on \r and \n). All pure functions over strings, fully unit-tested.
expectedBehavior: BranchParser.parse(...) returns [Branch] with ahead/behind extracted from upstream:track.
LogParser.parse(...) returns [Commit] with parent SHA arrays preserved.
StatusParser.parse(...) returns StatusSummary { branch, ahead, behind, staged, modified, untracked }.
ForEachRefParser.parse(...) returns [sha: [Ref]] mapping.
DiffParser.parse(...) returns [FileDiff] with hunks.
ProgressParser handles `\r`-updated lines without duplicating events.
verificationSteps: swift test --filter "*Parser*"
Parser tests use real git output fixtures committed under Tests/GitTests/Fixtures/

features.description: Implement FSEventStream wrapper watching a repo's .git/ directory with 500ms coalescing. Emits a debounced `changed` signal. Properly releases FSEvents stream on deinit.
expectedBehavior: Starting a watcher on a path returns a handle.
Writes inside the watched .git/ directory produce a `changed` event within 500ms.
Stopping the watcher releases the FSEvents stream (no leaks under Instruments).
verificationSteps: swift test --filter FSWatcherTests (writes to tmp dir, asserts callback)
Run Instruments → Leaks on the unit test

features.description: Implement UserDefaults + Codable persistence for settings. Add Persistence.Keys static constants under nl.rb2.kite.* prefix. Support: pinned repos list, extra root paths, last-opened repo path, last-selected branch per repo, window frame, split widths, auto-fetch enabled flag. Include schema version and a no-op migration hook.
expectedBehavior: Settings struct encodes/decodes via JSONEncoder/JSONDecoder stored in UserDefaults.
Reads/writes are synchronous and thread-safe (UserDefaults guarantees).
Schema version field present; missing-key defaults returned gracefully.
Unit tests round-trip every stored type.
verificationSteps: swift test --filter PersistenceTests
Manually toggle a setting, quit the app, relaunch, confirm preserved

features.description: Implement RepoScanner that enumerates directories under configured roots (depth-1 by default) and checks each for a .git/ entry. Handles bare repos by detecting HEAD + objects/ pattern. Runs off main actor, hops back on completion.
expectedBehavior: Scanning ~/Developer returns every directory with a .git/ subdirectory.
Non-git directories are excluded.
Bare repos are returned with a `bare: true` flag.
Scan completes in <200ms for 100 directories on M1+.
Invalid root path surfaces an error, does not crash.
verificationSteps: swift test --filter RepoScannerTests
Manually launch Kite and confirm ~/Developer repos appear

features.description: Implement repo sidebar: `NavigationSplitView` leftmost column shows pinned repos at the top, then repos grouped by root (collapsible sections). Shows repo name + relative path tooltip. Selecting a repo sets the focused repo in the AppModel.
expectedBehavior: Repos list in sidebar matches scanner output.
Pinned repos group appears above scanned groups.
Clicking a repo updates the detail pane to that repo within 500ms.
Empty state shows ContentUnavailableView with 'Add folder…' button opening Settings.
Last-selected repo restored on relaunch (via M1-persistence).
verificationSteps: xcodebuild test -scheme Kite -only-testing KiteUITests/RepoSidebarTests
Snapshot test renders both light and dark

features.description: Settings scene (⌘,) with tabs: General / Roots / About. Roots tab lets user add/remove root paths via `NSOpenPanel`. Each root shows its absolute path and a 'Scan now' button. Invalid (non-existent) paths are flagged inline.
expectedBehavior: ⌘, opens Settings window with three tabs.
Roots tab lists configured roots; add/remove updates persistence and sidebar immediately.
Adding a non-existent path shows an inline warning; does not crash.
Removing a root removes its repos from sidebar without restart.
verificationSteps: xcodebuild test -scheme Kite -only-testing KiteUITests/SettingsRootsTests
Manual: add ~/rb2-work and confirm its repos appear

features.description: Implement the branch list view (middle column, upper pane). Shows local branches (refs/heads/*) and grouped remote branches (refs/remotes/<remote>/*), each with a pill showing ahead/behind counts or 'no upstream'/'gone'. Current branch is highlighted. Detached HEAD shown as a pseudo-row.
expectedBehavior: All local branches listed, current branch marked.
Remote branches grouped by remote, collapsible.
Ahead/behind counts rendered accurately.
[gone] branches show a 'gone' badge.
Detached HEAD rendered as '(detached @ <short-sha>)'.
HEAD symbolic refs (e.g. origin/HEAD) filtered out.
verificationSteps: xcodebuild test -scheme Kite -only-testing ViewTests/BranchListTests
Snapshot tests for local/remote/detached/gone states
Manual on a fixture repo

features.description: Repo detail header shows working-tree summary (X modified, Y staged, Z untracked) from git status --porcelain=v2 parsing. Updates on FSEvents refresh.
expectedBehavior: Header renders current branch name + X/Y/Z counts.
After external `git add` in terminal, header updates within 2s.
Clean working tree shows 'clean'.
verificationSteps: ViewTests/StatusHeaderTests
Manual FSEvents refresh check

features.description: Wire up the focused-repo lifecycle: when the selected repo changes, start FSEvents watcher on its .git/, fetch branch list + status + log, cancel any in-flight tasks from previous repo. Per-repo GitQueue actor serializes git ops for the same repo.
expectedBehavior: Switching repos cancels in-flight ops on the previous repo and starts fresh ones.
Two simultaneous ops on the same repo run sequentially.
Closing the window terminates every active git Process.
verificationSteps: swift test --filter GitQueueTests
Manual: trigger two fetches quickly, confirm serial execution via logs

features.description: Implement the column-reuse graph layout algorithm with first-parent preference. Input: [Commit] (topo-ordered). Output: [LayoutRow] with column assignments, in-edges, out-edges. Implemented as a pure function + tested against a canonical fixture repo.
expectedBehavior: First parent stays in the same column when possible.
Columns are freed when a branch merges in, reassigned to later commits.
Octopus merges (≥3 parents) use straight-line fallback.
Output matches the committed reference JSON for the fixture repo.
verificationSteps: swift test --filter GraphLayoutTests
Reference fixture comparison via GraphLayoutTests.testFixtureRepo

features.description: Implement the per-row SwiftUI Canvas view drawing a single LayoutRow's commit dot + in/out edges for its Y range. Lane colors from the palette (name-hash → 6-color, main hardcoded blue). 3-segment straight/diagonal edges.
expectedBehavior: Commit dot is drawn at the commit's column.
In-edges (from this commit's parents above) and out-edges (to children below) drawn with correct lane colors.
main / master / trunk / default hardcoded to the blue slot regardless of hash.
Same branch renders same color across refreshes.
verificationSteps: Snapshot tests for 3-5 canonical row shapes (single-parent, merge, branch-off, octopus).
Manual scroll test on fixture repo with 200 commits.

features.description: Add the right-of-graph content in each row: commit subject, author name, relative age (TimelineView for live updates), and branch pills for any refs pointing at the commit. +N collapse for >3 pills.
expectedBehavior: Each row shows: graph column | subject | author | relative age | pills.
Pills render for local branches, remote branches; NOT tags (deferred).
+N overflow for >3 pills.
Relative age updates minute-by-minute via TimelineView.
verificationSteps: ViewTests/GraphRowTests snapshot
Manual: confirm 'main' and 'origin/main' pills visible on fixture repo

features.description: Container view embedding GraphRow in a List, with scroll preservation across refresh, '200 commit limit' footer, shallow-clone banner, and a selection binding that opens the diff pane on commit click.
expectedBehavior: Graph list scrolls at 60fps with 200 commits on M1+.
FSEvents refresh preserves scroll position.
Footer row shows '200-commit limit reached' when log cap hit.
Shallow repo shows top banner 'history truncated'.
Clicking a commit sets selection, routes to diff pane.
verificationSteps: Instruments Core Animation capture for 60fps verification.
KiteUITests/GraphScrollTests for scroll-preservation and footer.
Manual on a shallow repo: confirm banner.

features.description: Implement toast banner infrastructure: bottom-center toasts, 5s auto-dismiss for success (green), sticky for errors (red) with ✕ close button. Click error toast to open a detail panel showing full stderr. Toolbar progress indicator (indeterminate + determinate modes).
expectedBehavior: Showing a success toast dismisses after 5s.
Error toasts are sticky until clicked or dismissed.
Error detail panel shows full captured stderr.
Toolbar indeterminate spinner during unparseable-progress ops.
Toolbar determinate bar when ProgressParser yields percentage.
verificationSteps: Snapshot tests for each toast state.
ViewTests/ToastTests for auto-dismiss timing.
Manual: click error toast to expand stderr.

features.description: Wire up ⌘⇧F / toolbar Fetch button to Git.stream(["fetch", "--all", "--prune"]) on the focused repo. Parse progress stderr, drive the toolbar progress indicator, and surface completion/errors via toasts. Ahead/behind recomputed after completion.
expectedBehavior: Fetch command runs the documented args in the repo cwd.
Progress is visible during network transfer.
Success toast on completion; error toast with actionable message on auth failure or network error.
After successful fetch, branch list ahead/behind updates.
verificationSteps: KiteUITests/FetchTests with a fixture repo + local bare remote.
Manual: fetch a real repo, confirm progress + completion.

features.description: Wire up ⌘⇧P / Pull button (git pull --ff-only) and ⌘⇧K / Push button (git push, never --force). Surface non-fast-forward, auth, and no-upstream errors with actionable toasts. On missing upstream for push, offer confirm-to-run-with-upstream-set.
expectedBehavior: Pull runs `git pull --ff-only`; non-FF produces documented sticky error toast.
Push runs `git push` without force flags.
Missing-upstream error on push triggers a sheet offering `git push -u origin <branch>`.
Auth failures produce a sticky toast with setup suggestions.
verificationSteps: KiteUITests/PushPullTests with fixture diverged repo.
Grep for 'force' in source returns 0 results outside tests/docs.
Manual: push + pull on a real repo.

features.description: Background auto-fetch every 5 min on the currently focused repo. Timer cancels on repo change, window blur, window close, and can be disabled in Settings > General.
expectedBehavior: Timer fires `git fetch --all --prune` on focused repo every 5 min.
Switching repos cancels the previous timer.
Non-focused repos are never fetched in the background.
Settings toggle enables/disables auto-fetch globally.
verificationSteps: swift test --filter AutoFetchTimerTests (stubbed clock).
Manual: leave a repo open 5+ min with a known upstream change, confirm update.

features.description: ⌘⇧N opens a 'New branch' sheet with name validation (git ref-format rules). On confirm, runs `git switch -c <name>` from current HEAD. Error handling for duplicate name, invalid name, and dirty working tree.
expectedBehavior: Sheet validates name inline; invalid names are rejected with specific message.
Duplicate branch triggers sticky error toast.
On success, branch list updates with the new branch as current.
Dirty working tree error produces the documented 'stash or commit in terminal' toast.
verificationSteps: swift test --filter BranchNameValidatorTests
KiteUITests/CreateBranchTests
Manual: create a branch, confirm switch + pill update.

features.description: Double-click a local branch to `git switch <name>`. Double-click a remote branch to create a tracking local (`git switch -c <local> --track <remote>`) unless one already exists, in which case switch to it. Dirty-tree errors surface with same toast as M6-create-branch.
expectedBehavior: Double-click local branch switches to it.
Double-click remote branch creates tracking local + switches.
Existing local tracking the remote is chosen over creating duplicate.
Dirty working tree error surfaces consistently with create-branch flow.
Current indicator and graph update correctly after switch.
verificationSteps: KiteUITests/SwitchBranchTests with fixture repo + remote.
Manual: switch between branches, confirm HEAD + current indicator.

features.description: Diff pane (right column) 'Working copy' mode: runs `git diff` (unstaged) and `git diff --staged` (staged), merges into a single unified-diff view with file-path headers. Read-only. Binary files show 'Binary file — not displayed'. Empty tree shows a clean-state placeholder.
expectedBehavior: Working copy diff renders unstaged then staged, each under its own header.
Line coloring: + green, - red, context gray, no syntax highlighting.
Clean tree shows empty state.
Binary files do not render gibberish.
No staging / discard / revert actions exist anywhere.
verificationSteps: Snapshot tests for clean, mixed, binary, no-newline-at-eof cases.
KiteUITests/DiffReadOnlyTests confirming absence of action buttons.
Manual on a fixture with mixed staged/unstaged changes.

features.description: Diff pane 'Selected commit' mode: selecting a commit in the graph runs `git show <sha>` and renders the full commit diff (header: sha, author, date, subject, body; then diff content). Large diffs (>5k lines) virtualized via LazyVStack.
expectedBehavior: Selecting a commit renders its diff within 500ms.
Commit metadata shown above the diff.
10k-line diff scrolls at 60fps, memory < 200MB.
verificationSteps: Snapshot test for a medium commit diff.
Instruments capture for a 10k-line diff.
Manual: click a few commits, confirm rendering.

features.description: Wire up the App menu and keyboard commands via Commands/CommandGroup: ⌘R refresh, ⌘⇧F fetch, ⌘⇧P pull, ⌘⇧K push, ⌘N new window, ⌘, settings, ⌘⇧N new branch, ⌘T switch branch. Each command respects focused repo state (disabled when no repo selected).
expectedBehavior: All documented shortcuts invoke the right action.
Menu bar shows bindings next to each item.
Commands disabled/enabled based on selection state.
⌘N opens a second independent window.
verificationSteps: KiteUITests/CommandsTests exercising every shortcut.
Manual: open menu, confirm all items labeled + bound.

features.description: Design and add app icon (.icns in Assets.xcassets). Finalize Info.plist keys: CFBundleIdentifier=nl.rb2.kite, CFBundleName=Kite, LSApplicationCategoryType=public.app-category.developer-tools, LSMinimumSystemVersion=15.0, NSHighResolutionCapable=true. Add About window content.
expectedBehavior: Icon visible in Dock, Finder, About window at every size.
plutil -p Info.plist shows every required key.
About window shows name, version, credit line.
verificationSteps: shell: plutil -p Kite.app/Contents/Info.plist
Manual: observe icon in Dock and Finder after copy to /Applications.

features.description: Produce a reproducible Release build via `xcodebuild -scheme Kite -configuration Release`. Target: Kite.app under 20MB. Sign to Run Locally identity. Document a manual install flow (drag to /Applications, right-click Open first time). Audit for leftover Process on quit.
expectedBehavior: Release xcodebuild exits 0.
Kite.app size < 20MB.
App launches from /Applications, sandbox off, can execute /usr/bin/git.
On ⌘Q, no child git processes remain (pgrep -P <kite-pid>).
verificationSteps: xcodebuild -scheme Kite -configuration Release build
du -sh Kite.app
Manual: drag to /Applications, launch, fetch a repo, quit, pgrep.

coverage.notes: Every VAL-* assertion appears in exactly one feature's fulfills array, with a few load-bearing assertions (VAL-NET-010, VAL-REPO-005, VAL-BRANCHOP-006, VAL-GRAPH-009, VAL-PKG-004) fulfilled across two features where multiple flows contribute evidence. VAL-SEC-002, VAL-SEC-003, VAL-SEC-006, VAL-SEC-007 are grep/audit-level invariants fulfilled implicitly by scrutiny review and listed explicitly there. VAL-UI-001 fulfilled by M1-project-scaffold initial shell.
